headline_drivers: Brazil’s Petrobras decides not to exercise its waiver/right of first refusal over the sale of Novonor’s controlling stake in Braskem, effectively clearing the way for a third‑party buyer (Reuters, 2026-04-23)

Reports that Novonor has agreed to sell its Braskem stake to private equity group IG4 and partners, reviving the long‑running Braskem M&A story and raising expectations for a control transaction at a premium to the undisturbed price (Reuters, 2026-04-20)

Follow‑through buying and technical re‑rating as traders reposition around an event‑driven, takeover‑speculation name: BAK shows a 1‑day move of ~28%, volume >5x 10‑day average, and an RSI in the 97th percentile, consistent with a momentum/short‑covering squeeze layered on top of the M&A headlines (volume spike event logged 2026-05-12)
summary: The stock’s sharp move is being driven primarily by a renewed M&A/control-change narrative, not by a sudden improvement in fundamentals. In late April, Petrobras said it would not exercise its waiver over Novonor’s Braskem stake, and reports that Novonor agreed to sell that stake to IG4 effectively reopened the door to a control transaction that investors hope will come at a premium to the prior trading range. Against a backdrop of weak earnings and still‑pressured petrochemical margins, that strategic overhang clearing is what the market is repricing. The latest 1‑day surge looks like an acceleration of that re‑rating, amplified by event‑driven flows, momentum buying, and likely short covering on very high volume rather than by new operational data.
